prompt:
You are a B2B prospecting analyst for a web agency that sells digital solutions to local service businesses.

Analyze this locksmith's homepage for digital maturity and gaps. You are NOT judging quality — you are identifying sales opportunities.

Critical for locksmiths: a locked-out person on their phone needs to reach help in under 30 seconds.

HOT LEAD signals:
- No click-to-call button visible above the fold
- No service area mentioned
- No emergency availability stated (24/7)
- Slow load (critical for panicked mobile users)
- No trust signals (license, insurance, reviews)
- Generic template with stock photos
- No schema markup

COLD LEAD signals:
- Prominent emergency phone number, click-to-call
- Service area clearly listed
- 24/7 availability stated
- Fast mobile load
- Trust badges: license, insurance, trade body membership
- Real photos of vans, team, work
- LocalBusiness schema with service area

Return each record as:
{
  "url": "<page url>",
  "pageName": "<short name>",
  "summary": "<1-2 sentence overview of digital presence>",
  "opportunityScore": "<1-10, higher = more gaps = better lead>",
  "digitalMaturityScore": "<1-10, higher = more mature>",
  "hasClickToCall": true/false,
  "hasServiceArea": true/false,
  "hasEmergencyInfo": true/false,
  "hasTrustSignals": true/false,
  "hasMobileExperience": true/false,
  "topOpportunity": "<single biggest gap a web agency could sell>",
  "notes": "<one sentence prospecting assessment>"
}

---
You are analyzing web pages for the category described above.
For each page provided below, extract one record.
Follow the JSON schema from the category description exactly.

## Required fields (always include)
- "url": the page URL
- "pageName": short human-readable page name
- "summary": 1-2 sentence plain-language overview

## Field naming conventions (follow strictly)
- Scores: name ends with "Score", value is a number 1-10 (e.g. "conversionScore": 7)
- Booleans: name starts with "has"/"is" or ends with "Visible" (e.g. "pricingVisible": true)
- Short text: "notes", "description" — 1-2 sentences max
- Tags/lists: arrays of short strings (e.g. "classStyles": ["Hatha", "Vinyasa"])
- Plain strings: everything else (e.g. "price": "€15 drop-in")

Return a JSON object: { "records": [<one record per page>] }.
No markdown, no code fences.
response: {"records":[{"url":"https://www.london-locksmiths.com/index.htm","pageName":"London Locksmiths Homepage (index.htm)","summary":"An established London-wide emergency locksmith service with 24/7 availability, a freephone number, and extensive area coverage pages, but built on a visibly dated HTML template lacking modern mobile optimisation, trust signals, and structured data. The click-to-call is present but implemented as a GIF image link, and no licence, insurance, or customer review signals are displayed.","opportunityScore":7,"digitalMaturityScore":3,"hasClickToCall":true,"hasServiceArea":true,"hasEmergencyInfo":true,"hasTrustSignals":false,"hasMobileExperience":false,"topOpportunity":"Full mobile-first redesign with proper tap-to-call button, LocalBusiness schema markup, and visible trust signals (licence number, insurance, reviews) to convert panicked mobile users before they bounce to a competitor.","notes":"Operationally well-structured with strong area coverage and 24/7 messaging, but the ancient table-based template, GIF click-to-call, zero reviews, and no schema markup make this a strong candidate for a modernisation pitch."},{"url":"https://www.london-locksmiths.com","pageName":"London Locksmiths Homepage (root)","summary":"Identical content to the index.htm page — a dated static HTML site for a London emergency locksmith with 24/7 coverage and a freephone number, but no modern mobile UX, no trust badges, no schema, and a click-to-call implemented as a legacy GIF rather than a native button. Service area breadth is strong but credibility signals are entirely absent.","opportunityScore":7,"digitalMaturityScore":3,"hasClickToCall":true,"hasServiceArea":true,"hasEmergencyInfo":true,"hasTrustSignals":false,"hasMobileExperience":false,"topOpportunity":"Full mobile-first redesign with proper tap-to-call button, LocalBusiness schema markup, and visible trust signals (licence number, insurance, reviews) to convert panicked mobile users before they bounce to a competitor.","notes":"Same opportunity profile as the index.htm entry — this business has the operational depth to justify a serious digital upgrade and would benefit significantly from a modern, conversion-focused site."}]}
